content.full_text:
27202

CHAMBERS OF

The Superior Court

LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90012

EUGENE E. SAX, JUDGE

TELEPHONE
(213) 974-1234

November 19, 1976

SEX                                A 76-449

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attention: Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

Re: Your circular letter dated November 10, 1976
    to County Election Officials, et cetera re
    Filing Requirements for Judges Under the
    Political Reform Act

Gentlemen:

     I am a judge of the Superior Court sitting in Los
Angeles County. I have not been a candidate for election
during this year, having been elected in 1972 for a six
year term expiring in 1978.

     Please send me the necessary forms for Statement of
Economic Interests which I understand are to be filed some-
time in 1977.

     Concerning the Commission staff recommended January
31, 1977 as the closing filing date, it is my suggestion
that this be reconsidered for the following reasons:

1.   In January, right after the holiday season, many judges
     will endeavor to file their 1976 income tax returns and
     gathering data therefor during that time.

2.   Many judges will be starting new and different assign-
     ments in the first of the year and will have all they
     can do to learn and apply new procedures and deal with
     new problems.

                                    sent form 731-
                                    12-8-76
                                    L.W.

[illegible] 0 [illegible] = [illegible]

[The rest of the document appears to be blank or too faint/illegible to transcribe accurately. There is a circular stamp/seal visible in the upper right corner, but the text within it is not clearly legible.]

State of California
Fiar Political Practices Commission
November 19, 1976

Page Two

3.     The matter of possible conflict of interest of a judge
has never been a problem in this State, as experience
has shown. The provisions of the California Code of
Civil Procedure, commencing with Section 170, the
California Code of Judicial Conduct, and the authority
of the Commission on Judicial Qualifications all provide
sufficient measures to effectively prevent any judge
from sitting in any case which conflicts with his
economic interests.

I therefore suggest that March 15, 1977 be selected as
the closing date for filing of Statements of Economic Interests.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

EBS:8
cc: Hon. Robert A. Wenke, Presiding Judge
     Hon. William P. Hogoboom, Assistant
          Presiding Judge

This image appears to be a blank or nearly blank white page with some very faint marks or artifacts, possibly from scanning or copying. There is no clearly legible text content to transcribe.

Concerning the Commission staff recommended January 31, 1977 as the closing filing date, it is my suggestion that this be reconsidered for the following reasons: 1.
